lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Bob, seventy candles glow
Soft as dawn on Lake Conroe
Forty years in Houston heat
Steel and dirt beneath your feet

[Pre-Chorus]
You carried home so much more
Than the tired hours you wore
Three small hearts grew strong and fine
On the work you gave in time

[Chorus]
We love you, Bob, so much
We love you, Bob, so much
For the years you gave, for the hands you held
For the life you built so well
We love you, Bob, so much
We love you, Bob, so much
(so much)

[Verse 2]
Pipe by pipe, you kept it true
Every mile you pushed right through
Now your rod bends in the breeze
On that quiet water ease

[Pre-Chorus]
Sun on the line, boat on the lake
Peace can find you when you wake
And every cast, it seems to say
You made a whole good world this way

[Chorus]
We love you, Bob, so much
We love you, Bob, so much
For the years you gave, for the hands you held
For the life you built so well
We love you, Bob, so much
We love you, Bob, so much
(so much)

[Bridge]
Now the hard days soften low
Like old gold in evening glow
Three kids stand with grateful eyes
And our love just keeps on rising

[Final Chorus]
We love you, Bob, so much
We love you, Bob, so much
For the years you gave, for the hands you held
For the life you built so well
We love you, Bob, so much
We love you, Bob, so much
Happy birthday, love
Happy birthday, love
title: Conroe Morning
